Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.

end of world model.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# I've been wasting actions pressing SPACE. Let me try to understand the maze navigation.
# The player is at rows 8-12, cols 14-18. Let me check the maze layout around the player.

ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("Player area and surroundings (rows 6-16, cols 12-22):")
for r in range(6, 17):
    print(f"  Row {r}: {ascii_lines[r][12:22]}")

# Check what's below the player
print("\nBelow player (rows 12-20, cols 14-18):")
for r in range(12, 21):
    print(f"  Row {r}: {ascii_lines[r][14:18]}")

# Try moving DOWN to see what happens
predict(expect_change=True, note='Test DOWN movement from start')
action(['DOWN'])

# Check player position after DOWN
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['hash'] == 'fa982fde30da5312':
        print(f"\nPlayer boundary after DOWN: {n['boundary']}")
        break

# Check what changed
if transitions:
    last = transitions[-1]
    b_lines = last.before_frame.ascii.split('\n')
    a_lines = last.after_frame.ascii.split('\n')
    diffs = [(r,c,b_lines[r][c],a_lines[r][c]) for r in range(63) for c in range(64) if b_lines[r][c]!=a_lines[r][c]]
    print(f"DOWN changed {len(diffs)} cells")
    for d in diffs[:15]:
        print(f"  [{d[0]},{d[1]}]: '{d[2]}' -> '{d[3]}'")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

    b_lines = last.before_frame.ascii.split('\n')
    a_lines = last.after_frame.ascii.split('\n')
    diffs = [(r,c,b_lines[r][c],a_lines[r][c]) for r in range(63) for c in range(64) if b_lines[r][c]!=a_lines[r][c]]
    print(f"DOWN changed {len(diffs)} cells")
    for d in diffs[:15]:
        print(f"  [{d[0]},{d[1]}]: '{d[2]}' -> '{d[3]}'")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
